Article Title: From red to green to saffron? BJP eyes power shift in Bengal as Mamata defends her turf
Article Text:
Watch Mamata Alleges EVM Manipulation, Claims Obstruction At Strong Room; Warns Of ‘Life And Death’ Fight Mamata Alleges EVM Manipulation, Claims Obstruction At Strong Room; Warns Of ‘Life And Death’ Fight

Congress era: Foundations of power and post-Partition rebuilding

The red era: Rise and consolidation of Left dominance

The Mamata moment: Disruption and dominance

BJP's surge: From fringe to formidable force

2026 showdown: Continuity or change?





NEW DELHI: The Banglar 'manush' have traditionally displayed a deep-rooted political loyalty, often rallying decisively behind a single political force and allowing it to govern for extended periods. Over nearly eight decades since independence, West Bengal's political journey has not merely been a sequence of governments changing hands, but a story shaped in clear, dominant eras, each marked by ideological conviction and prolonged mandates.The 2026 assembly elections, however, have injected a new intensity into this legacy. What is unfolding is not just another electoral contest, but a high-stakes, high-voltage battle between long-incumbent chief minister Mamata Banerjee and a determined, aggressively expanding Bharatiya Janata Party brigade.Didi's 15-year rule now faces its most formidable challenge yet. The BJP has mounted a relentless, ground-up campaign, deploying its top leadership and sharpening its attacks on the Trinamool Congress . The saffron party's high command has gone so far as to assert that "the sun has set for TMC in Bengal."But to understand the weight of BJP's claim, the story of how Bengal's political journey has evolved must be told.Like most states in newly independent India, Bengal's early political leadership was shaped by the Congress.Bidhan Chandra Roy, one of the most important icons in the state's history, became its first chief minister at a time when Bengal was reeling from the devastating effects of Partition in 1947.Bidhan Chandra's leadership extended beyond routine governance. He played a crucial role in stabilising a fractured society and laid the foundations for modern Bengal's administrative and infrastructural framework.Among his lasting contributions was the development of Salt Lake city, a planned township that symbolised forward-looking urbanisation.Kolkata, then Calcutta - was not just the state capital but also a political nerve centre for the Congress party. The city hosted several landmark sessions of the party across decades, becoming a stage for defining moments in India’s freedom struggle and political evolution.From the first singing of Vande Mataram to the articulation of Swaraj, Bengal remained at the heart of Congress.However, the Congress's dominance gradually began to erode. Internal fractures, shifting political aspirations, and national-level upheavals weakened its grip. The emergence of the Bangla Congress marked the first serious rupture in its monopoly, introducing coalition politics and opening the door for alternative ideologies.The deaths of towering national leaders like Jawaharlal Nehru and Lal Bahadur Shastri further destabilised the party's structure and also its influence. The 1967 elections, often described as a "political earthquake," dealt a decisive blow to Congress supremacy in Bengal.Even as Indira Gandhi strengthened her political authority at the national level, especially after the 1971 electoral victory and the India-Pakistan war, Bengal was moving in a different direction.The Congress continued to lose ground in the state, and its final chief minister to the date, Siddhartha Shankar Ray, came to represent the end of an era. The political vacuum was swiftly filled by the Left Front, which rose to power in 1977 on the back of strong grassroots mobilisation and ideological clarity.At the centre of this transformation was Jyoti Basu, a towering Marxist leader who would go on to become one of India's longest-serving chief ministers. Under his leadership, Bengal witnessed a profound restructuring of its rural and administrative systems.Key initiatives such as Operation Barga redefined land relations by strengthening the rights of sharecroppers, while the institutionalisation of the Panchayati Raj system in 1978 decentralised governance and empowered local bodies.These reforms created a durable political base for the Left.The Left Front governed Bengal uninterrupted from 1977 to 2011, one of the longest continuous tenures for any democratically elected communist government in the world.By 2000, Basu stepped down due to health reasons, passing the baton to comrade Buddhadeb Bhattacharjee. While Bhattacharjee attempted to recalibrate the Left’s image with a focus on industrialisation, the ground realities had begun to shift.Discontent was brewing, and a new political force under Mamata Banerjee was gathering momentum.Mamata emerged as new strong leader in state.Emerging as a fierce and relentless challenger, Mamata dismantled the Left's decades-long dominance in the 2011 assembly elections. Leading the Trinamool Congress, she secured a sweeping mandate of 184 seats and became Bengal's first woman chief minister.Her victory marked not just a change in government, but a complete political reset. The Congress and the Left,once the principal forces in the state, were pushed to the margins.The BJP, despite having governed at the Centre earlier under Atal Bihari Vajpayee for nearly 6 years, remained an insignificant player in Bengal politics at the time.Mamata strengthened her grip further in 2016, returning to power with an even bigger mandate, crossing the 200-seat mark. At this stage, her dominance appeared unchallenged, with opposition forces fragmented and struggling to regain relevance.Her governance style, welfare outreach, and strong personal connect with voters ensured that the Trinamool Congress remained the central pole of Bengal politics.The political equilibrium began to shift dramatically in 2021.The BJP launched an unprecedented campaign in Bengal, led by its stars Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union home minister Amit Shah, also considered as "Chanakya" for the saffron party. The scale, intensity, and resources deployed marked a new phase in the state’s electoral politics.While Didi retained power, the BJPs performance signalled a major breakthrough. Winning 77 seats in the 294-member Assembly, the party emerged as the principal opposition, leapfrogging boththe Congress and the Left.The BJP's strong emergence, in fact, pushed Congress and Left to the farthest margins of insignificance.This rise was significant not just numerically, but symbolically. A party that once struggled to make electoral inroads in Bengal had now positioned itself as the primary challenger to the ruling establishment under TMC supremo Mamata.Now, as Bengal records one of its highest-ever voter turnouts and witnesses intense, high-decibel campaigning, the stakes have never been higher.Is the state signalling a shift? Has “the sun has set for TMC in Bengal,” as the BJP leadership claims?Also the traditional notion of high voter-turnout interpreted as an undercurrent against the incumbent.The contest has increasingly taken on a bipolar character, squeezing out other political players and turning the election into a direct face-off between the Trinamool Congress and the BJP.The BJP, buoyed by its 2021 gains, has upped the ante, fielding heavyweight candidates even in traditional TMC bastions like Bhowanipore and other constituencies in southern Bengal.As a clear messaging - no seat is being conceded without a fight.Several pollsters in their exit poll predictions have also given an edge to BJP over Mamata's TMC.This might be the first signal of discomfort for Didi. The poll of polls predicted that TMC would no longer be single strong and leading force, as BJP taking over it.In 2021, Mamata faced her former aide and a BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari in Nandigram and suffered a rare personal defeat, even as she retained power across the state.This time, the dynamics appear more complex, with both sides locked in a strong contest from Bhowanipore.The elections have also seen a massive administrative exercise, including the special intensive revision process, criticised by Mamata but defended by the Election Commission, adding another layer of political tension.Will it continue its tradition of backing one dominant force for the long haul, or will it script a new chapter by embracing change?The answer lies with the Banglar 'manush'.
Article Summary: The 2026 assembly elections have injected a new intensity into this legacy. What is unfolding is a high-stakes, high-voltage battle between long-incumbent chief minister Mamata Banerjee and a determined, aggressively expanding Bharatiya Janata Party brigade. Mamata's 15-year rule now faces its most formidable challenge yet.

Article Title: It’s a numbers game
Article Text: Thirukkural with the Times explores real-world lessons from the classic Tamil text ‘Thirukkural’. Written by Tamil poet and philosopher Thiruvalluvar, the Kural consists of 1,330 short couplets of seven words each. This text is divided into three books with teachings on virtue, wealth, and love and is considered one of the great works ever on ethics and morality. The Kural has influenced scholars and leaders across social, political, and philosophical spheres. Motivational speaker, author and diversity champion Bharathi Bhaskar explores the masterpiece. There are minds to whom numbers feel like weight—symbols to be endured and then forgotten. And then there are minds to whom every number carries a quiet radiance. To such a mind, nothing is trivial; even an ordinary number can unfold into wonder. In 1918, when G H Hardy visited the ailing Srinivasa Ramanujan , he mentioned, almost casually, that the taxi he came in bore the number 1729 — a rather dull number. Ramanujan, with instinctive clarity, disagreed. No, it was deeply interesting: the smallest number that can be expressed as the sum of two cubes in two different ways — 1729 = 1³ + 12³ = 9³ + 10³. What seemed dull to one mind revealed symmetry to another. Perhaps that is the nature of numbers; they do not announce their beauty; they wait to be seen. And yet, many do not like maths. One popular myth claims that Alfred Nobel excluded mathematics from his prestigious prize list because he did not like it. The story goes further that it was due to his personal dislike for mathematician Magnus Gustav, rumoured to be his lover’s husband. Nobel was never married. However, the myth does not hold; there were other prizes already instituted for mathematics. When one dwells on the Thirukkural, a different kind of mathematical beauty begins to emerge— in numbers. One starts to notice the precision, the order and the mathematical temperament in the construction of each couplet. Most of us, at some point in school, have encountered ‘ conditional statements ’: (p → q)—if p is true, then q must be true. A simple example: if a number is divisible by 6, then it is divisible by 3. It is a familiar structure, used in proofs, quietly shaping our understanding of logic. That same structure appears, with striking consistency, in many couplets in the Kural. Take Kural 205: Ilanendru Theeyavai Seyyarka; Seyyin Ilanaagum Matrum Peyarthu “Do not do evil deeds because you are poor. If done, It will invite further impoverishment” The first line sets up the premise (p); the second delivers the consequence (q). It reads almost like a lived theorem: (p → q). Across the text, one encounters many such couplets—each a compact expression of cause and effect, where life itself is arranged with logical clarity. Beyond individual verses lie another layer of mathematical elegance—what one may call a quiet “sequencing theory.” The chapters unfold not randomly, but with deliberate progression. “Kalvi” (learning) is followed by “Kallaamai” (ignorance), then “Kelvi” (listening), and then “Arivudaimai” (wisdom). It feels like a structured ascent: to learn, to avoid ignorance, to listen, and to arrive at wisdom. The order is not incidental; it is instructive. Not to mention the intact mathematical precision in the structure of the couplets, seven words, ten couplets per chapter, three parts, 133 chapters, 1,330 couplets. It is this terse structure that has ensured that the Thirukkural is one of the rare literary works where people have not ‘inserted’ their own lines of poetries. I have always wondered how several ancient mathematicians were philosophers as well; Pythagoras, Archimedes, AryaBhatta and Brahmagupta. Valluvar also gilds this lineage of scholars who interspersed mathematics as a tool for searching the truths of cosmos. But he went one step ahead. He also wove all of these in the fine labyrinth of poetry. We are truly gifted to read Thirukkural way of enduring truth: that beneath the seeming randomness of the world lies a pattern — precise, elegant, and purely mathematical.
Article Summary: Bharathi Bhaskar explores real-world lessons from the classic Tamil text ‘Thirukkural’ Written by Tamil poet and philosopher Thiruvalluvar, the Kural consists of 1,330 short couplets of seven words each.

Article Title: West Bengal Elections: Today’s Chanakya predicts big win for BJP in West Bengal
Article Text:
Watch Record Bengal Turnout at 92.9%, SIR Row and Voter Surge Complicate Poll Reading Record Bengal Turnout at 92.9%, SIR Row and Voter Surge Complicate Poll Reading

NEW DELHI: Today’s Chanakya Thursday predicted a big win for BJP in West Bengal, while News18 in its exit poll has forecast a tight contest with an edge to BJP.In Tamil Nadu, Today’s Chanakya gave a majority to the DMK and its allies where actor Vijay-led TVK has emerged as an X factor that may push the AIADMK-BJP combine to third position.While most exit polls have given BJP an edge in West Bengal, Today’s Chanakya has forecast a massive lead for the party by giving it 48% votes compared to the governing TMC’s 38% with a margin of 3% for both parties on either side. It will translate into 192 (plus or minus 11) seats for BJP and 100 (plus or minus 11) for TMC in the 294-member assembly, it has said. News 18 has predicted 143 to 163 seats for BJP and 127 to 147 for TMC, with the two parties tipped to get 44% and 43% votes respectively.In Tamil Nadu, Today's Chanakya said, DMK’s alliance could get 125 seats and TVK 63 in the 234-member assembly. It has given an edge to UDF in Kerala and predicted a landslide victory for BJP in Assam, forecasts which are in line with the general direction of other polls. Axis My India, another pollster, decided not to release its forecast for West Bengal due to an “exceptionally high non-response rate”, with approximately 70% of approached voters declining to participate in the survey.“A refusal rate of this magnitude materially exceeds historical norms and introduces a high degree of non-response bias,” it said.
Article Summary: In Tamil Nadu, Today’s Chanakya gave a majority to the DMK and its allies. Actor Vijay-led TVK has emerged as an X factor that may push the AIADMK-BJP combine to third position. Record Bengal Turnout at 92.9%, SIR Row and Voter Surge Complicate Poll Reading

Article Title: Sabarimala Case: Can’t be party to annihilation of a religion: SC on Sabarimala case
Article Text:
Supreme Court of India

Matter of reli gion is a matter of conscience, not for debate: SC

New Delhi: Supreme Court on Wednesday said if a person's fundamental right to freedom of religion is held to be superior to the identical right of a group or denomination, it could lead to dangerous consequences and the court was not going to be part of the process for annihilation of a religion.Bindu Ammini, a lawyer and social activist who was manhandled for attempting to enter Sabarimala after the 2018 SC judgment removed the ban on entry of women in the 10-50 age group, asserted her fundamental right to enter a temple. Indira Jaising, appearing for Bindu and another woman Kanakadurga, said there was no theological bar on women entering any public temple.Appearing before a bench of Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justices B V Nagarathna, M M Sundresh, A Amanullah, Aravind Kumar, A G Masih, P B Varale, R Mahadevan and J Bagchi, advocate Indira Jaising said Bindu did not dispute the 'naistik brahmachari' attributes of Ayyappa at Sabarimala, but the custom could not be the ground for violation of her fundamental right to enter the temple.Jaising said that the Indian Constitution was hailed as unique across the world because it gave prominence to individuals' fundamental rights. "If a woman wants to go into a temple, what legal injury is she causing to anyone? If the court wants to rule the other way round, let it go ahead and do it and the world will be watching how the Supreme Court of India is developing jurisprudence relating to rights of women," Jaising said.Justice Sundresh disagreed with her line of argument and asked if an individual's right to freedom of religion under Article 25(1) clashed with that right of a group of devotees or followers of a denomination, whose right should prevail?"How do we enforce individual rights when it violates fundamental rights of others? Article 25(1) right of one cannot be pitted against another. If we agree with your submissions, it will lead to dangerous consequences. If each devotee goes to a common deity and exercises his freedom to worship in a different manner, the consequences will be disastrous for the religion or denomination itself," he said.Justice Nagarathna agreed with him and said, "It will lead to annihilation of religion, and we do not want to be a part of it. Matters of religion are not a subject on which either the court or the legislature can sit in judgment. It cannot be a matter of debate because it is a matter of conscience."Justice Amanullah asked whether a practice or custom, which has crystallised over centuries, should be removed by a court to ensure that a person must go into a temple despite knowing that it would hurt the religious feelings of the majority of the followers of the denomination. Arguments will continue on Thursday.
Article Summary: Bindu Ammini, a lawyer and social activist, was manhandled for attempting to enter Sabarimala after the 2018 SC judgment removed the ban on entry of women in the 10-50 age group. She asserted her fundamental right to enter a temple. Arguments will continue on Thursday.

Article Title: Caitlin Clark sends 3-word response mocking Indiana Fever’s PR nightmare following AI-assisted post
Article Text:
Caitlin Clark. Image via: John Jones-Imagn Images

Caitlin Clark calls out Indiana Fever's huge social media mishap



Caitlin Clark is set for Fever's regular-season game opener with Wings



Caitlin Clark gives a hilarious 3-word response mocking the Indiana Fever’s PR nightmare following an AI-assisted post that turned a routine team update into a moment fans could not ignore. The Indiana Fever had shared a social media graphic featuring Caitlin Clark and teammate Aliyah Boston, but it did not take long for viewers to notice something was off. Clark saw it too and responded in her own way.The post, which included a broadcast schedule graphic, quickly drew attention for an unusual detail in Clark’s hand. It looked distorted, clearly not natural. The image was later removed, but not before Clark dropped a quick comment that summed up the moment better than any explanation.Caitlin Clark kept it simple and sharp. “New hand alert,” she wrote under the post, a line that instantly spread across social media. It was not harsh, just honest and a little playful. That tone matched her personality, someone who does not shy away from a moment but rarely takes it too seriously.The mistake itself came from an attempt to blend two separate images using an AI tool, according to reports. The goal was to combine Clark and Boston into one clean graphic.Instead, the final product left an obvious flaw. The Fever quietly deleted the post after the reaction picked up.Across sports, teams are experimenting more with AI in creative work. This case showed both the upside and the limits. The technology can move fast, but it still needs a careful eye. Clark’s comment turned what could have been a simple slip into a light moment that fans embraced.While the social media moment grabbed attention, the focus soon returned to basketball. Clark is preparing to lead Indiana into the new season, starting with a matchup against the Dallas Wings on May 9 at Gainbridge Fieldhouse.Her preseason has already brought a brief scare. In a recent game against Dallas, Clark went down after contact and left the floor early. She later reassured reporters she was “fine,” easing any concern around her availability. The team has not placed any restrictions on her heading into the opener.There is a sense of anticipation around this group. Clark enters the season healthy after a disrupted previous year, and expectations are building again. The Fever will look to turn that energy into results, starting with a familiar opponent.
Article Summary: The Indiana Fever shared a social media graphic featuring Caitlin Clark and teammate Aliyah Boston. The post, which included a broadcast schedule graphic, quickly drew attention for an unusual detail in Clark’s hand. The image was later removed, but not before Clark dropped a quick comment.

Article Title: ‘Does not make for a pretty sight’: Jamieson’s fiery send-off to Sooryavanshi sparks outrage, debate erupts
Article Text:
Kyle Jamieson gives Vaibhav Sooryavanshi a fiery send-off. (Video grabs)

Watch Shikhar Dhawan on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, life after retirement and more Shikhar Dhawan on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, life after retirement and more

Early strikes set tone for DC

WATCH: Jamieson's fiery send-off

Rahul, Nissanka seal record chase

Fans reacted strongly to the send-off:

NEW DELHI: A fiery moment involving Kyle Jamieson and teenage prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi stole the spotlight during the IPL 2026 clash between Delhi Capitals and Rajasthan Royals in Jaipur on Friday, triggering a wave of reactions across the cricketing fraternity.Jamieson dismissed the 15-year-old Sooryavanshi for just 4 in the second over with a sharp yorker. While the delivery itself showcased skill and control, it was the celebration that followed that grabbed attention. The New Zealand pacer clapped aggressively and stared down the youngster, delivering a fiery send-off that left many surprised.Former India cricketer Priyank Panchal was quick to react, writing on X: “31-year-old Kyle Jamieson giving a 15-year-old kid a send off after dismissing him. Does not make for a pretty sight. But also shows how much of an impact and fear Vaibhav has instilled amongst the opponents already.”The incident came amid a dramatic start to the match. Mitchell Starc struck in the very first over, removing Yashasvi Jaiswal with a caught-and-bowled effort. Soon after, Jamieson’s dismissal of Sooryavanshi left Rajasthan reeling at 12 for 2.However, RR skipper Riyan Parag responded in style with a counterattacking 90 off 50 balls, silencing critics and steering his side to a competitive 225 for 6.He found crucial support from Dhruv Jurel (42), while Donovan Ferreira’s explosive 47 off just 14 balls added late impetus.Parag, who had been under pressure due to poor form and off-field scrutiny, showed glimpses of his best with a flurry of boundaries, including a stunning sequence against Jamieson and a tennis-like forehand six off T Natarajan.In reply, Delhi Capitals produced a batting masterclass. KL Rahul (75) and Pathum Nissanka (62) stitched a 102-run opening stand in just 9.3 overs, laying the foundation for a record chase.Rahul dazzled with elegant strokeplay, while Nissanka dominated with his pull shots. Despite a brief wobble after Rahul and Nitish Rana fell in quick succession, Ashutosh Sharma and Tristan Stubbs calmly guided DC to 226 for 3 — completing their highest-ever IPL chase.While DC celebrated a crucial win, Jamieson’s send-off remained a major talking point — highlighting both the intensity of competition and the growing aura of young Sooryavanshi.
Article Summary: Kyle Jamieson gives 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi a fiery send-off. The incident happened during the IPL 2026 clash between Delhi Capitals and Rajasthan Royals in Jaipur on Friday. The New Zealand pacer clapped aggressively and stared down the youngster. Former India cricketer Priyank Panchal was quick to react to the incident.

Article Title: Jeanie Buss reveals why Lakers kept Luka Doncic trade talk a secret and the reason involves Mark Cuban
Article Text:
Jeanie Buss and Luka Doncic. Image via: Kevork Djansezian/ Getty Images | Luke Hales/ Getty Images

Why Jeanie Buss and the Lakers kept the Luka Doncic trade a secret?



The Lakers and the Dallas Mavericks after the Luka Doncic trade



Jeanie Buss reveals why the Lakers kept Luka Doncic trade talk a secret, and the reason involves Mark Cuban , a decision that still shapes how this blockbuster deal is viewed across the league. More than a year later, the move that brought Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers continues to raise questions, not just about the trade itself but about how it stayed hidden for so long. The answer, as Buss explains, comes down to timing, control, and one influential voice that never got the chance to step in.Back in early January, discussions quietly began between the Lakers and the Dallas Mavericks. There were no leaks, no rumors, no signs that a generational player could be moved. Inside both organizations, only a handful of people knew. That silence proved to be the difference between a completed deal and one that might have collapsed before it ever reached the finish line.For Jeanie Buss, secrecy was not optional. It was essential. “The Dallas team wanted Anthony Davis . And he was on our team. So conversations started [in January], but it was important that they remained private and didn't leak out to the media, because if a player is made aware of a pending trade, it could crater your entire season...Rob Pelinka, our general manager, was able to do that all without leaking to the media. Because I think Mark Cuban might have jumped in front of a train to keep it from happening.”That last line speaks volumes. Mark Cuban still carried influence within Dallas, and Buss believed any hint of the deal reaching him could have changed everything. Internally, even players were kept in the dark. “Nobody knew, including Luka, Anthony Davis, and LeBron James,” Buss said, highlighting how tightly controlled the process was.She expanded further on why that level of discipline mattered. “It was really important to me that we didn't blow up the team. If it had leaked out and the trade hadn't happened, that would be really unfair to the progress that the coaching staff had made with the team. Because it's a huge distraction.”The Lakers pushed forward with Doncic as their centerpiece, climbing into the playoff picture and building around his all-around production. His arrival shifted the franchise’s timeline, giving them a younger star to lead the next phase.Dallas, on the other hand, struggled to stabilize after the move. Nico Harrison, who drove the deal, eventually paid the price as results failed to follow. The roster turned over, the direction changed, and the Mavericks found themselves resetting sooner than expected.
Article Summary: Jeanie Buss explains why the Lakers kept the Luka Doncic trade a secret. Buss believes Mark Cuban might have "jumped in front of a train to keep it from happening" The Lakers pushed forward with Luka as their centerpiece, climbing into the playoff picture.

Article Title: 'I don't need to answer critics': Riyan Parag's blunt take after Rajasthan Royals loss
Article Text:
Riyan Parag (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

Watch Shikhar Dhawan on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, life after retirement and more Shikhar Dhawan on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, life after retirement and more

NEW DELHI: Coming into the match after being fined 25% of his match fee and handed one demerit point for breaching Level 1 of the IPL’s Code of Conduct - for using a vape inside the dressing room - Riyan Parag ensured he put the controversy behind him when he walked out to bat against Delhi Capitals.The Rajasthan Royals captain looked in ominous touch, smashing a superb half-century. He appeared set for a well-deserved hundred but fell just short, scoring a brilliant 90 off 50 balls, including eight fours and five sixes, to power his side to 225/6 in 20 overs.However, despite the strong total, Delhi Capitals snapped their three-match losing streak with a commanding seven-wicket win.Parag, after the match, made it clear that outside criticism does not bother him, but his primary responsibility is to deliver results for the team."I don't think I need to answer any critics or anyone talking about it. But at the end of the day, my job is to get two points. So my score and my innings don't really matter if we lose the game," Parag said after the match.A blistering 110-run opening stand between KL Rahul (75 off 40) and Pathum Nissanka (62 off 33) laid the perfect foundation for a record 226-run chase, which was calmly finished by Tristan Stubbs (18*) and Ashutosh Sharma (25*) with five balls to spare."I think it was a good score. I thought 200 was a par score here. It was going to slow down a bit, but I think we could have bowled way better in the middle overs, not let them get away a little too much. And then a lot of boundaries in one single over and that repeated. So I felt we missed the trick with that, but no harm with the decision we made," Parag said.Parag had earlier been fined 25% of his match fee and handed a demerit point after he was caught vaping inside the dressing room during his side’s match against Punjab Kings.He admitted to the offence and accepted the sanction imposed by match referee Amit Sharma.Parag was found to have breached Article 2.21 of the IPL’s Code of Conduct, which relates to “conduct that brings the game into disrepute.” The incident occurred during the second innings when he was seen using a vape inside the dressing room.
Article Summary: Riyan Parag scores 90 as Rajasthan Royals beat Delhi Capitals by seven wickets. Parag had earlier been fined 25% of his match fee and handed a demerit point. He was caught using a vape inside the dressing room during his side’s match against Punjab Kings.

Article Title: Ashoka Road accident: Killer truck got special permission from Delhi traffic cops despite 5 challans
Article Text:
Ashoka Road accident

: No arrest made so far, raids on in Bihar

NEW DELHI: The truck that fatally hit two cousins on Ashoka Road in central Delhi had been granted "special permission" by Delhi Traffic Police to operate before no-entry hours for the collection of horticultural waste in the area. Such permissions are processed and approved by the DTP headquarters in west Delhi's Todapur, a senior police officer said.However, police sources pointed out that the offending vehicle had accumulated at least five challans, raising serious questions about how it qualified for the exemption. "As per norms, vehicles with three or more challans are generally not considered eligible for these permissions," said a source.The sources said the vehicle had been issued a one-year permit, valid until Dec 2026, allowing it to operate during restricted hours for specific municipal purposes, such as debris collection and garbage removal. They added that on the day of the incident, debris from the Jeevan Tara building on Ashoka Road was also being cleared.Asked how the vehicle was cleared despite multiple violations, a senior officer said at the time of application, it may have had only three challans on record.The remaining challans, he suggested, may have been registered after the permit had already been issued and the vehicle had begun operating under the exemption.In light of the incident and the vehicle's violation history, the special permission granted to the truck is now set to be cancelled," the officer added.No arrests have been made in the case so far. Police said the driver's last known location has been traced to Bihar and raids are currently being conducted there to locate and apprehend him.The incident took place on Monday when cousins Yagya and Abhav Bhatia were returning home after watching an IPL match. As they reached Ashoka Road, they were hit by the truck, resulting in their deaths.The family of the victims have alleged that they have not been adequately informed about the progress of the investigation. They claimed that despite repeated attempts to seek clarity, there has been little official communication regarding key developments in the case."We have not been given any clear updates about what the police have found so far. It has been nearly a week and there is still no arrest," said Ravi Bhatia, a relative of the victims. He added that the situation has left the entire family devastated.Bhatia said that the two cousins were the only children of their parents. "The absence of any concrete progress in the investigation has only deepened their grief, as the family continues to wait for answers and accountability," he added.
Article Summary: The truck that fatally hit two cousins on Ashoka Road in central Delhi had been granted "special permission" by Delhi Traffic Police. Such permissions are processed and approved by the DTP headquarters in west Delhi's Todapur. Police sources pointed out that the offending vehicle had accumulated at least five challans, raising serious questions about how it qualified for exemption.

Article Title: NHL trade rumors intensify as Auston Matthews and William Nylander pushes Toronto Maple Leafs toward significant roster upgrades this offseason
Article Text:
Auston Matthews and William Nylander (Image Via Twitter)

Auston Matthews and William Nylander highlight the Toronto Maple Leafs NHL team priorities

Amidst what was described as exit interviews, Auston Matthews and William Nylander reportedly discussed key issues regarding the roster with the Toronto Maple Leafs, potentially entering what could easily be considered one of the most impactful NHL offseasons the team has ever experienced. Darren Dreger, an insider, reported that both stars requested that the management make the team tougher and more mobile on the offensive side, as the team had yet another dismal season.Such discussions that have been reported have also enhanced NHL trade rumors regarding the franchise. The leadership team in Toronto seems less interested in including more offensively oriented and playmaking forwards and defensemen with their pucks since the management contemplates redefining their roster around Matthews and Nylander and entering the next season.Auston Matthews and William Nylander allegedly said to the Toronto Maple Leafs that they require tougher forwards and movable defensemen.The remarks are allegedly made in team-exit meetings following the challenging season enjoyed by Toronto. According to Darren Dreger , both players do not lose hope in the core of the roster; however, they believe that the team is physically outmuscled and that they do not have an assuring blue line that moves the pucks.These concerns are representative of some of the problems that Toronto has had to deal with over the season, particularly the major games that had the shape of playoffs.The fact that Matthews and Nylander determined the same weaknesses is noteworthy since it provides the management with the first-hand evaluation internally held by two franchise stars.The demand for “dogs up front" (possibly) indicates that Toronto can get more powerful power forwards or veterans of two-ways out of the NHL offseason. In the meantime, the post requesting two mobile defensemen suggests that the organization might become a party to NHL trade rumors and free agency in the next several months.The front office of Toronto is now under pressure. Some speculation about the long-term prospects of Auston Matthews is also quieted in the report.
Article Summary: Auston Matthews and William Nylander reportedly said to the Toronto Maple Leafs that they require tougher forwards and movable defensemen. Darren Dreger, an insider, reported that both stars requested that the management make the team tougher and more mobile on the offensive side.

Article Title: Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua clash hits unexpected hurdle as Dua Lipa decision sparks new concerns
Article Text:
Dua Lipa, Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua (Image Via Getty)

Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua fight uncertainty grows as Eddie Hearn responds to Dua Lipa Wembley situation

The long-awaited fight between Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua has taken another unexpected turn, and this time it has nothing to do with boxing. Reports suggest the mega fight could face problems after singer Dua Lipa turned down an offer to perform at Wembley Stadium. The idea was reportedly linked to plans around the event, which has already been years in the making.Right now, the fight is still expected to happen, but this situation has raised fresh questions. The two fighters had finally agreed to face each other after a long back and forth over the years. Now, with new details coming out about conditions around the event, fans are once again left wondering if everything will go as planned.As the story picked up attention, Anthony Joshua’s promoter Eddie Hearn quickly addressed the situation. He made it clear that neither Joshua nor Fury had asked for any such condition. Speaking this week, he said, “That’s not a request from either fighter or me. [Turki] he is saying that if Netflix want to do the show it’s got to be in London and Dua Lipa’s got to perform. That’s over to Turki Alalshikh.”It is still not fully clear how serious that condition was, or if it was said in a light way.But the report has still created doubt around the fight, especially given the history between Fury and Joshua.This is not the first time their fight has faced issues. Back in 2021, their planned bout collapsed after Deontay Wilder secured a trilogy fight with Fury through a legal ruling. Then again in 2023, talks restarted after Fury publicly called out Joshua, but both sides could not agree on the final terms.Things seemed different this time. After Fury’s recent win over Arslanbek Makhmudov, he openly called out Joshua in front of the crowd. He said, “I want to give you the fight you’ve all been waiting for. I want you, AJ, Anthony Joshua. Let’s give the fight fans what they want, the Battle of Britain. I want to fight you next, do you accept my challenge?”Joshua responded in a calm way, saying, “I’ve sat at this table many times. I’m not here to chase clout. Contracts will be sent over and I’ll more than likely be in the ring with him next.”Before that, Joshua is set to face Kristian Prenga on July 25 in a warm up fight. It will be an important moment for him, especially after a difficult period in his personal life. Last year, he was involved in a serious car accident in Nigeria that led to the loss of two close friends. He survived with minor injuries and has since returned to training.For now, the Fury vs Joshua fight is still on track, but once again, small details could decide whether it finally happens.
Article Summary: Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua fight uncertainty grows as Eddie Hearn responds to Dua Lipa situation. Reports suggest the mega fight could face problems after singer Dualipa turned down an offer to perform at Wembley Stadium. The idea was reportedly linked to plans around the event, which has already been years in the making.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumor: Dallas Cowboys, Green Bay Packers, and Cleveland Browns linked to New Orleans Saints running back Alvin Kamara
Article Text:
NFL Trade Rumor: Dallas Cowboys, Green Bay Packers and Cleveland Browns linked to New Orleans Saints running back Alvin Kamara (Image via Getty: Alvin Kamara)

New Orleans Saints RB Alvin Kamara in trade talks with Cowboys, Packers, and Browns

Washington Commanders, Indianapolis Colts, and Cincinnati Bengals tied to Alvin Kamara

Saints running back linked to Kansas City Chiefs and Cleveland Browns

ESPN’s Mike Clay reported on X that Arizona Cardinals RBs James Conner and Trey Benson and New Orleans Saints RB Alvin Kamara “need new homes.”And Mike Clay sees them fit for the Washington Commanders, Indianapolis Colts, Dallas Cowboys, Cincinnati Bengals, Green Bay Packers, Kansas City Chiefs, and Cleveland Browns.Our point of concern? Alvin Kamara for the Dallas Cowboys, the Cleveland Browns, and the Green Bay Packers.As for the Dallas Cowboys? Jerry Jones’ franchise has Javonte Williams penciled in as the starter at running back. But behind him? They have Malik Davis, Jaydon Blue, and Phil Mafah.Bringing in Alvin Kamara could make a lot of sense. He has been a dual threat, recording 7,000-plus rushing yards, 4,900-plus receiving yards, and 86 touchdowns in 126 games.Heavy Sports’ Evan Massey suggests that Alvin Kamara has hinted at the possibility of retirement if the New Orleans Saints traded him away. But Kamara could be an interesting veteran option for the Dallas Cowboys. He could form a solid one-two punch alongside Javonte Williams in the backfield.As for the Green Bay Packers? Heavy Sports’ Evan Massey suggests the Green Bay Packers could consider Alvin Kamara as an elite option for a backup role behind Josh Jacobs.They could consider trading for Alvin Kamara. The asking price may not be too high at this stage of his career.The 30-year-old’s 2026 salary was reduced from $18 million to $10 million by the New Orleans Saints using the CBA’s 50% rule. Also, the Saints signed Jacksonville Jaguars running back Travis Etienne Jr. to a four-year, $52 million contract, which averages $13 million per year.Now, let’s eliminate the listed trade competitors for Alvin Kamara: Washington Commanders? They drafted Kaytron Allen, they signed Rachaad White, and Jerome Ford. So, they don’t need any running back as such.Indianapolis Colts? Now, they could be competition for the Dallas Cowboys and the Green Bay Packers. Because Colts Wire’s Paul Bretl reports that the Indianapolis Colts need a running back. They have Jonathan Taylor. But he needs backup because of injury concerns.They have DJ Giddens and Seth McGowan competing for a backup role. But the duo has limited NFL experience. Signing a veteran could bring stability in pass protection and on special teams.As for the Cincinnati Bengals? According to The Sporting News’ Billy Heyen, talented running backs Jamal Haynes and Kentrel Bullock have been signed by the Cincinnati Bengals as undrafted free agents.As for the Kansas City Chiefs? They signed a Super Bowl-winning running back, Kenneth Walker III, and they drafted a Nebraska Cornhuskers running back in the fifth round as their 161st overall pick, Emmett Johnson.As for the Cleveland Browns? According to 247Sports’ Jack Duffin, the Cleveland Browns need a running back, and they have not added anyone so far. This could mean they heavily depend on 23-year-old Raheim Sanders for 2026. Though his stats do not signify much confidence [58 snaps and 27 carries].Sports Illustrated’s Brian Schaible links Alvin Kamara to the Cleveland Browns. Quinshon Judkins is the centerpiece. Dylan Sampson showed flashes and had a couple of receiving touchdowns. But he’s still adapting to the role.That’s where New Orleans Saints running back Alvin Kamara comes into the picture. He gives the quarterback, whether Shedeur Sanders or Deshaun Watson , an outlet where there is nothing. “He can turn short throws into real gains and keep drives alive,” said Sports Illustrated’s Brian Schaible.That leaves us with, who would sign Alvin Kamara for his 2026 chapter? Or would he retire? Remains to be seen…
Article Summary: New Orleans Saints running back Alvin Kamara has been linked to a number of NFL teams. The Washington Commanders, Indianapolis Colts, Dallas Cowboys, Cincinnati Bengals, Green Bay Packers, Kansas City Chiefs, and Cleveland Browns are some of the teams linked to the 30-year-old.

Article Title: Vanessa Bryant shares painful birthday message for Gianna as Kobe Bryant memories return and 20 scholarships revealed
Article Text:
Vanessa Bryant and Gianna Bryant (Image via Getty)

Vanessa Bryant and Gianna Bryant’s legacy lives on through scholarships and family tributes

The day was filled with love and pain for Vanessa Bryant as she marked what would have been her daughter’s 20th birthday. She shared a quiet but emotional post on Instagram, reminding everyone how much she still feels her daughter’s presence, even after all these years. The message was simple, but it carried deep emotion. “Happy birthday to my sweet baby angel, Gianna,” she wrote, alongside a photo of them together. “Words can’t express how much I love and miss you mamacita. Mommy loves you so much!”.The post quickly connected with fans who still remember the young basketball player’s bright future. Gianna Bryant passed away in January 2020 in a helicopter crash with her father, Kobe Bryant . They were on their way to a basketball game in California. She was only 13. The loss shook the sports world and continues to be remembered.This birthday was not just about remembrance. It also became a moment to celebrate Gianna’s dreams. The Mamba & Mambacita Sports Foundation announced something meaningful on the same day. In a video shared online, the foundation revealed that 20 scholarships were given to young athletes in Gianna’s name.The announcement said, “In Gigi’s honor, we are proud to announce the 2026 Class of Gianna Bryant Scholars, 20 exceptional student-athletes from Los Angeles and Orange County, administered in partnership with the California Community Foundation and local Boys & Girls Clubs of America affiliates.”The message ended with, “And to our Gigi, Happy 20th Birthday! Your vision, your spirit, and your beautiful heart continue to light the way. ”Vanessa has kept her daughter’s memory alive in many ways. She often shares family moments with her daughters Natalia, Bianka, and Capri. On this birthday, Natalia Bryant also posted photos of herself with Gianna and wrote, “Happy Birthday Gigi.”Just days earlier, Vanessa had shared photos with Kobe to mark their 25th wedding anniversary. She also remembered him on his birthday last year with another heartfelt message, writing, “We love and miss you and Gigi so much. Sending our love to you. Happy birthday, baby.”Through these moments, Vanessa continues to keep both Kobe and Gianna close, not just for her family but for fans around the world.
Article Summary: Vanessa Bryant marked what would have been her daughter's 20th birthday. Gianna Bryant passed away in January 2020 in a helicopter crash with her father, Kobe Bryant. The Mamba & Mambacita Sports Foundation announced 20 scholarships in Gianna's name.

Article Title: Luke Littler challenges Mo Farah to wild half marathon with 501 darts twist as talks begin to heat up
Article Text:
Luke Littler and Mo Farah (Image Via Getty)

Luke Littler backs himself against Mo Farah in half marathon with darts twist as both stars react to viral challenge

A strange but fun challenge is now picking up attention, and it involves two very different sports coming together. Luke Littler has said yes to a viral idea where he could face Mo Farah in a half marathon. But this is not just about running. The twist is that both of them would need to hit a 501 checkout on a dartboard after every mile.The idea sounds unusual, but it has already caught the interest of fans and even other athletes. Littler spoke about it after his latest Premier League Darts win in Aberdeen. While he seemed excited, he also admitted he would need to get fitter before taking it on. At the same time, Mo Farah has also shown interest, which makes this challenge feel closer to becoming real.Luke Littler gave a very honest and relaxed response when asked about the challenge. He said, “I don’t think Mo Farah could take out 501 personally. So as soon as I am done, nine darter, I am walking my next mile. Yeah, I think we should do it for charity. Obviously let me lose a bit of timber and I’ll be good to go.”His confidence comes from his strength in darts, even though Farah is one of the best long-distance runners in the world.The format would make things interesting. After each mile, both would stop and try to finish a 501 score. That means focus and skill will matter just as much as stamina.Mo Farah, who is now 43, has also reacted positively. Responding to the idea, he said, “I will let my team get in touch with you! Yeeeezzzzzz!! 501.” That short reply was enough to show he is open to the challenge.Farah has a strong background in running. He won Olympic gold in both the 5,000 meters and 10,000 meters at the London 2012 and Rio 2016 Games. His personal best in a half marathon is 59 minutes and 22 seconds, which shows how strong he is over long distances.On the other side, Littler is enjoying a great season in darts. His recent win in Aberdeen was his fifth Premier League victory this season. That result has pushed him to the top of the standings, and he has already secured his place in the playoffs.The idea for this challenge also got support from boxer Dave Allen. Speaking on talkSPORT, he said he even tried to contact Farah to help make it happen.If the event goes ahead, both players would need to complete 13 successful 501 checkouts during the race. That mix of running and darts could make it one of the most unique sporting events fans have ever seen.
Article Summary: Luke Littler has said yes to a viral idea where he could face Mo Farah in a half marathon. The twist is that both of them would need to hit a 501 checkout on a dartboard after every mile. The idea has already caught the interest of fans and even other athletes.

Article Title: Are Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce new part-owners of Kentucky Derby contender The Puma? Mystery deepens after NDA hint
Article Text:
Rumors suggest Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce may own a stake in Kentucky Derby contender The Puma, but nothing is confirmed. Speculation grew after co-owner Michael Iavarone said he could “neither confirm nor deny” due to an NDA. The buzz boosted the horse’s popularity and odds, with fans and bettors closely watching as the Derby approaches.

Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Kentucky Derby rumors gain traction

Michael Iavarone’s cryptic post fuels Swift Kelce speculation

The speculation surrounding Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce has taken an unexpected turn, this time in the high stakes world of horse racing. What began as a murmur of online gossip soon became a roar after a big player linked to a Kentucky Derby hopeful jumped in with both feet. Now fans are watching closely, their curiosity turning into buzz.The intrigue centers on The Puma, a Derby upstart who has surprisingly become the subject of celebrity talk. While there is no official confirmation tying Swift and Kelce to the horse, a cryptic response from co-owner Michael Iavarone has only deepened the mystery and kept fans guessing.The rumor mill kicked into high gear earlier this week when posts began circulating online claiming Swift and Kelce had quietly secured a stake in The Puma. One widely shared message read, “Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift are now 10 percent part owners of Kentucky derby entry The Puma.” That single claim was enough to ignite intense interest, especially among Swift’s massive fan base.And as the chatter continued, more folks started to make the connections. Swift and Kelce’s dabbling in the sport of horse racing added another layer to their already headline-grabbing lives.But in the absence of confirmation, the possibility was enough to make The Puma one of the most talked about names ahead of the race.The conversation reached another level when Iavarone addressed the rumors directly, though without offering clarity. In a message shared online, he wrote, “Ok people. Everyone asking me if Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are partners on The Puma…” before adding, “All I can say is I signed an NDA so I can’t confirm or deny.” The statement, paired with a playful tone, left fans reading between the lines.That ambiguity had a real impact. The Puma’s odds saw a sharp rise midweek, moving from around 10-1 to 7-2 before stabilizing. Some believe the surge was driven by Swift’s fanbase, known for rallying behind anything connected to the pop icon. As one sports commentator noted, “Rumor has it that Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have bought into The Puma. The Swifties could make this horse a top choice on Saturday.”Whether the rumors prove true or not, the buzz has already changed the narrative. The Puma is no longer just a contender. It is now part of a larger cultural moment where sports, celebrity, and speculation collide.
Article Summary: Rumors suggest Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce own a stake in Kentucky Derby contender The Puma. Speculation grew after co-owner Michael Iavarone said he could “neither confirm nor deny” due to an NDA. The buzz boosted the horse’s popularity and odds, with fans and bettors closely watching as the Derby approaches.

Article Title: "Look into it": Dianna Russini scandal explodes as NFL urged to probe A.J. Brown Patriots link
Article Text:
Pablo Torre has urged the NFL to review Dianna Russini’s role in linking A.J. Brown to the New England Patriots. While the league views the Russini-Mike Vrabel situation as personal, Torre raised concerns about optics and potential scrutiny. The NFL has no plans to investigate, but attention could grow if trade talks involving Brown move forward.

Dianna Russini A.J. Brown Patriots rumors raise NFL questions

Watch Pablo Torre on Brendan Sorsby, Dianna Russini, and Soup Throwing Pablo Torre on Brendan Sorsby, Dianna Russini, and Soup Throwing

NFL stance on Mike Vrabel Dianna Russini situation remains unchanged

The noise around Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel is no longer just a personal controversy. It is starting to overlap with football decisions, trade chatter, and league integrity questions. What once felt like off-field drama is now brushing up against on-field implications, and that shift has caught the attention of media voices across the NFL landscape.At the center of the latest debate is A.J. Brown and his rumored link to the New England Patriots. The speculation alone would have been enough to spark headlines. But when it intersects with an ongoing controversy involving reporting, relationships, and access, the conversation becomes far more complicated.The discussion took a sharper turn when Pablo Torre publicly questioned whether the league should take a closer look. Speaking about Russini’s role in earlier reporting tied to Brown’s potential move, Torre said, “I think the NFL should look into it.” His concern was not framed as an accusation but as a question about oversight.He added, “But I also think that this is really interesting. And my governor for whether you should look into something is, is it interesting? The NFL’s governor on whether they should look into this is, is this a business problem for us that we need to attend to? And for them, it is absolutely not.So there is just the practical reality of that.”The comments reflect a broader unease. Russini had been among the most visible voices linking Brown to New England, and now, with personal controversy surrounding her and Vrabel, some believe the optics deserve scrutiny. It is not about proving wrongdoing. It is about whether the situation creates enough doubt to warrant a second look.Despite the growing chatter, the league has made its position clear. Commissioner Roger Goodell addressed the issue directly, stating, “This is not a personal conduct policy… as we know it today.” He continued, “It’s a personal matter, and we’ll leave it at that. I think the teams handle these matters when they’re personal matters, and they have a lot more information that can benefit everyone involved.”That stance effectively shuts down any immediate investigation. Still, the conversation is not going away. If a trade involving Brown materializes, especially after June, the spotlight will only grow brighter.For now, the Patriots remain focused on building their roster, while the Eagles weigh their options. But hovering above it all is a lingering question. When personal stories begin to intersect with professional outcomes, where should the league draw the line?
Article Summary: Pablo Torre has urged the NFL to review Dianna Russini’s role in linking A.J. Brown to the New England Patriots. Torre raised concerns about optics and potential scrutiny. The NFL has no plans to investigate, but attention could grow if trade talks involving Brown move forward.
